title: Cleft Sentences — Emphasis
description: IT-clefts and WH-clefts shift emphasis onto one part of the sentence.
summary_pt_br: Cleft sentences: 'It is X that Y' / 'What I need is Y' para enfatizar. Ênfase, contraste ou correção.

scenes.subtitle_en: IT-clefts and WH-clefts shift emphasis onto one part of the sentence.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Cleft sentences: 'It is X that Y' / 'What I need is Y' para enfatizar. Ênfase, contraste ou correção.

scenes.text_en: IT-clefts and WH-clefts shift emphasis onto one part of the sentence. Today's focus: cleft sentences — emphasis. It was Maria who finally solved the puzzle.
scenes.text_pt: Cleft sentences: 'It is X that Y' / 'What I need is Y' para enfatizar. Ênfase, contraste ou correção. Foco de hoje: cleft sentences — emphasis. Foi a Maria que finalmente resolveu o enigma.

scenes.title: Cleft Sentences — Emphasis
scenes.body_en: IT-cleft: 'It is X that/who Y.' → 'It was Maria who solved the puzzle.' WH-cleft: 'What I need is Y.' → 'What I need is a holiday.' Use for emphasis, contrast, or correction.
scenes.body_pt: Cleft sentences: 'It is X that Y' / 'What I need is Y' para enfatizar. Ênfase, contraste ou correção.

examples.en: It was Maria who finally solved the puzzle.
examples.pt: Foi a Maria que finalmente resolveu o enigma.

examples.en: What I really need is a long holiday.
examples.pt: O que eu realmente preciso é de umas férias longas.

examples.en: It was in 2015 that we moved to Brazil.
examples.pt: Foi em 2015 que nos mudamos para o Brasil.

exercise.question: Complete: "It was Maria who ___ solved the puzzle."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "It was Maria who finally solved the puzzle."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Foi a Maria que finalmente resolveu o enigma.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
What I really need is a long holiday.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "What I really need is a long holiday."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O que eu realmente preciso é de umas férias longas.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Cleft Sentences — Emphasis — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre cleft sentences — emphasis. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Who finally solved the grammar question yesterday?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was Maria who solved the puzzle first.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What does the class really need right now?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What we need is more practice with speaking.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: When did you realize teaching was your calling?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was during my first class that I knew.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Where did you train to become a teacher?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was in London where I studied linguistics.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Which lesson do you enjoy teaching most?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What I love teaching most is pronunciation.
turns.pt_hint: O que mais amo é pronúncia.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Clefts make every answer sound deliberate.

scenes.title: The moment that changed everything
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize Zara's story. Include at least one IT-cleft and one WH-cleft.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Zara often told her students about the single lesson that had turned her into a teacher.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: It was a rainy Tuesday in March when I realized teaching was my true calling.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: What did your first class think of you as a brand-new teacher?

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: What they liked most was that I made my own mistakes in front of them.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And it was in that moment that you decided to stay in Brazil, right?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Clefts in grammar, like pivots in life, take the focus exactly where it belongs.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Cleft Sentences — Emphasis". IT-clefts and WH-clefts shift emphasis onto one part of the sentence. It was Maria who finally solved the puzzle. What I really need is a long holiday. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Cleft Sentences — Emphasis". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Cleft Sentences — Emphasis".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
